• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

This file is used to generate :

  • sample_info : dataframe containing sample names, sample path, custom colors…
  • color_markers (short version) : named list to give custom colors to cell types
  • cell_markers (short version) : named list containing markers for each cell type
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
.libPaths()
## [1] "/shared/projects/nf1_scrnaseq_r/R_packages/R_3_6_3"            
## [2] "/shared/ifbstor1/software/miniconda/envs/r-3.6.3/lib/R/library"

Preparation

In this section, we set the global settings of the analysis. We will store data there :

save_name = "Tom_metadata"
out_dir = "."

Sample info

We initialize a dataframe containing everything except colors :

sample_info = data.frame(
  project_name = c("2023_17","2023_18","2023_21","2023_27","2023_32","2023_34","2023_36","2023_40","2023_48","2023_44","2023_46"),
  exp_date = c(rep("23_05_04",2),
               rep("23_05_31",1),
               rep("23_06_15",1),
               rep("23_08_08",1),
               rep("23_08_10",2),
               rep("23_09_07",1),
               rep("23_10_16",1),
               rep("23_10_12",2)),
  age = c(rep("P365",4), 
          rep("P90",3), 
          rep("P365",1), 
          rep("P90",3)),
  genotype = c(rep("flfl", 2),
               rep("ctrl", 2),
               rep("ctrl", 1),
               rep("flfl", 3),
               rep("ctrl", 1),
               rep("flfl", 2)),
  condition = c(rep(c("HLS","cNFs","HS","HS","HS","cNFs","HLS","HLS","HS","cNFs","HLS"),1)),
  color = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_type = paste0(genotype, "_", condition, "_", age)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(unique_id = data.table::rowid(sample_type)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_identifier = paste0(exp_date, "_", age, "_", condition)) %>%
  dplyr::select(project_name, exp_date, genotype, age, condition, sample_identifier, sample_type, color)

head(sample_info)
##   project_name exp_date genotype  age condition  sample_identifier
## 1      2023_17 23_05_04     flfl P365       HLS  23_05_04_P365_HLS
## 2      2023_18 23_05_04     flfl P365      cNFs 23_05_04_P365_cNFs
## 3      2023_21 23_05_31     ctrl P365        HS   23_05_31_P365_HS
## 4      2023_27 23_06_15     ctrl P365        HS   23_06_15_P365_HS
## 5      2023_32 23_08_08     ctrl  P90        HS    23_08_08_P90_HS
## 6      2023_34 23_08_10     flfl  P90      cNFs  23_08_10_P90_cNFs
##      sample_type color
## 1  flfl_HLS_P365    NA
## 2 flfl_cNFs_P365    NA
## 3   ctrl_HS_P365    NA
## 4   ctrl_HS_P365    NA
## 5    ctrl_HS_P90    NA
## 6  flfl_cNFs_P90    NA

We add custom colors for each sample :

list_palette = list(flfl_HLS_P90 = colorRampPalette(c("paleturquoise2", "skyblue1")),
                    flfl_cNFs_P90 = colorRampPalette(c("darkorange", "sienna3")),
                    ctrl_HS_P90 = colorRampPalette(c("darkolivegreen1", "yellowgreen")),
                    flfl_HLS_P365 = colorRampPalette(c("deepskyblue", "deepskyblue4")),
                    flfl_cNFs_P365 = colorRampPalette(c("firebrick1", "firebrick3")),
                    ctrl_HS_P365 = colorRampPalette(c("chartreuse3", "forestgreen")))

for (sample_type in unique(sample_info$sample_type)) {
  matching_dataset = (sample_info$sample_type == sample_type)
  sample_info[matching_dataset, "color"] = list_palette[[sample_type]](sum(matching_dataset))
}

We visualize the colors :

sample_info = sample_info %>%
  dplyr::arrange(as.character(sample_identifier)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(project_name = factor(project_name, levels = project_name)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_identifier = factor(sample_identifier, levels = sample_identifier))

ggplot2::ggplot(sample_info) +
  ggplot2::geom_label(aes(x = project_name, y = 0, label = project_name, fill = project_name)) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(aes(x = project_name, y = 1, label = sample_identifier), angle = 10) +
  ggplot2::lims(y = c(-1, 2)) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = sample_info$color, breaks = sample_info$project_name) +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")

It is possible to make confusion between colors ?

graphics::pie(rep(1, nrow(sample_info)),
              col = sample_info$color,
              labels = sample_info$project_name)

We save it :

saveRDS(sample_info, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sample_info.rds"))

Cell types

We define markers for each population of interest :

cell_markers = list(
  "Myelinating schwann cells" = c("Sox10", "Plp1", "S100b", "Lgi4", "Mpz","Pmp22","Mal","Egr2"),
  "Non myelinating schwann cells" = c("Sox10", "Plp1", "S100b", "Lgi4","Gap43","Ngfr","Scn7a"),
  "Immune cells" = c("Ptprc","Xcr1","Cpa3","Tpsab1","Hdc","Tpsb2"),
  "Fibroblasts" = c("Abi3bp","Ramp1","Mylk","Acta2", "Tagln","Grem2","Pdgfra","Dcn" ,"Sparc","Col3a1","Lum","Cyp2f2","Ccl19" , "Gpx3","Gsn","Crabp1","Pappa2","Corin", "Nrg2","Cntn1","Plac9","Antxr1","Clmp"),
  "Mural cells" = c("Acta2","Rgs5", "Notch3", "Gucy1a1", "Mylk", "Gm13889", "Tagln", "Myh11","Des"),
  "Endothelial cells" = c("Fabp4", "Pecam1", "Cldn5", "Ptprb", "Egfl7"),
  "Melanocytes" = c("Mlana", "Dct"),
  "Keratinocytes" = c("Krt27","Krt28","Krt10","Krt15","Krt14","Krt1","Epcam","Krt5","Cdh1","Gm15987","Abca12"),
  "Muscle cells" = c("Pax7","Myod1","Acta2","Des"))

lengths(cell_markers)
##     Myelinating schwann cells Non myelinating schwann cells 
##                             8                             7 
##                  Immune cells                   Fibroblasts 
##                             6                            23 
##                   Mural cells             Endothelial cells 
##                             9                             5 
##                   Melanocytes                 Keratinocytes 
##                             2                            11 
##                  Muscle cells 
##                             4

Here are custom colors for each cell type :

color_markers = aquarius::c30_palette[c(1:length(cell_markers))]
names(color_markers) = names(cell_markers)

data.frame(cell_type = names(color_markers),
           color = unlist(color_markers)) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(., aes(x = cell_type, y = 0, fill = cell_type)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(pch = 21, size = 5) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = unlist(color_markers), breaks = names(color_markers)) +
  ggplot2::theme_classic() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none",
                 axis.line = element_blank(),
                 axis.title = element_blank(),
                 axis.ticks = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10))

We save them :

saveRDS(color_markers, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_color_markers.rds"))
saveRDS(cell_markers, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_cell_markers.rds"))
